data.title: BlackRock bought $1.15 billion worth of Bitcoin this week
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to Cointelegraph statistics, BlackRock purchased $1.15 billion worth of Bitcoin this week, bringing its holdings to a record high of $77.7 billion.

data.title: More than $2.1 billion in crypto assets were stolen in the first half of 2025, 70% of which came from North Korean-related hacker groups
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the TRM Labs report, the cumulative losses in the crypto field due to hacker attacks in the first half of 2025 exceeded US$2.1 billion, of which about US$1.6 billion (70%) were carried out by North Korean-related organizations. The largest case was the US$1.5 billion robbery against Bybit in February, setting a record high. The report pointed out that more than 80% of the stolen funds came from infrastructure-level intrusions, including private key theft and front-end hijacking. In addition, on June 18, another organization named GonjeshkeDarande launched a targeted attack on the Iranian exchange Nobitex, with an amount of approximately US$90 million, indicating that national-level cyber operations are exacerbating industry security risks. (Coincu)

data.title: SharpLink Gaming purchased another 1,989 ETH through OTC, worth $4.82 million
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: SharpLink Gaming previously announced the purchase of 188,478 ETH (US$473.6 million) at a price of US$2,513, and purchased another 1,989 ETH (US$4.82 million) through OTC 5 hours ago.

data.title: Binance Alpha is in decline, with yesterday's trading volume of $487 million, down 70% from its high point
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to Dune data, Binance Alpha's trading volume reached US$487.07 million on June 27, which remained at a low level. On June 8, Binance Alpha's daily trading volume peaked at US$2.04 billion, and then began to decline. Today's daily trading volume has dropped by more than 70% from its peak.

data.title: web3 game company SpekterGames completes $5 million in Pre-seed round of financing, led by a16zSpeedrun
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Spekter Games announced the completion of a $5 million pre-seed round of financing, led by a16zSpeedrun, with participation from LondonVenturePartners, BRVCapital, Chamaeleon, AcceleratorVentures, Impact46, VersusVentures and AlumniVentures.
This financing is synchronized with the official release of Spekter Games' first game Spekter Agency, a rogue-lite action game that will be launched on Telegram. (BusinessWire)

data.title: Ripple and SEC end legal battle, withdraw appeals in landmark crypto case
data.description:
28-6-2025 – The protracted legal dispute between Ripple Labs and the United States Securities and Exchange Commission has reached its denouement, with both parties electing to abandon their respective appeals rather than pursue further litigation. In a momentous development that draws the curtain on one of the cryptocurrency industry’s most closely watched legal battles, Chief [. .
source: https://www.cointrackdaily.com/ripple-sec-lawsuit-appeals-withdrawn/

data.title: Citi UK Official Questions 1,250% Crypto Risk Weight as Market Growth Accelerates
data.description:
In recent crypto news, Citi UK’s Tiina Lee raised concerns over a 1,250% risk weight on crypto assets. It could exclude banks from the growing digital asset sector, she warned. Speaking at TheCityUK’s London summit, Lee urged regulators to reconsider the restrictions
source: https://www.thecoinrepublic.com/2025/06/27/citi-uk-official-questions-1250-crypto-risk-weight-as-market-growth-accelerates/

data.title: Whale Trader AguilaTrades Increases BTC Position to $160 Million
data.description: According to BlockBeats, on-chain analyst Ai Yi (@ai_9684xtpa) has reported that whale contract trader AguilaTrades has increased their BTC position to $160 million. As of early today, the trader holds 1,500 BTC with an entry price of $107,288.9 and a liquidation price of $101,730. The current unrealized loss stands at $635,000.

The address has accumulated 5,089 ETH in the past week, with a total value of US$12.65 million and an average price of US$2,486.

data.title: Cryptocurrency Market Sees Mixed Performance with Notable Gains and Losses
data.description:
According to PANews, the cryptocurrency market experienced varied movements on June 28, with several tokens showing significant gains and others facing declines. Leading the day's gains, token W is currently priced at $0.0683, marking a 7.82% increase. XRP follows with a price of $2.175, up by 3.69%. ZRO is reported at $1.75, showing a 1.63% rise, while CFX is at $0.0718, increasing by 1.60%. NEAR also saw a positive movement, priced at $2.12 with a 1.48% gain.

Conversely, some tokens experienced notable losses. LPT is currently priced at $5.947, reflecting a 7.03% decrease. AAVE stands at $257.72, down by 2.73%. ARB is reported at $0.306, showing a 1.79% decline, while RPL is priced at $4.876, decreasing by 1.77%. ILV also saw a downturn, priced at $10.22 with a 1.64% drop.

data.title: The VIRTUAL whale that previously received a $1.61 million airdrop sold its positions but still lost $6.54 million
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the monitoring of on-chain analyst Yu Jin, a whale/institution that previously used 10 million USDC to buy VIRTUAL, after receiving a VVV airdrop worth 1.61 million US dollars, ended up losing 6.54 million US dollars. The operation process is as follows: In December last year, he spent 10 million USDC to buy 4.251 million VIRTUAL at an average price of 2.35 US dollars; In January, he received an airdrop of 101,000 VVVs for holding a large number of VIRTUALs, and then sold the airdrops for 1.61 million USDC; In March, 4.251 million VIRTUALs were sold at an average price of 0.56 US dollars. Today, he cleared the address and transferred 3.46 million USDC to Coinbase.

data.title: Ripple abandons cross-appeal against SEC and accepts $125 million fine, and the long-term legal battle may come to an end
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Ripple Labs announced that it would withdraw its cross-appeal against the U.S. SEC and would implement the relevant provisions of the previous court ruling. This decision means that the multiple rounds of legal battles since the SEC filed a lawsuit against Ripple in 2019 may come to an end.
Giving up the appeal means that Ripple will accept the initial $125 million civil penalty and may retain a permanent ban. In 2023, Judge Torres ruled that XRP sales to retail investors on public exchanges did not constitute securities transactions, but sales to institutional investors violated securities laws. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Ripple CEO Announces End to SEC Legal Battle
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Ripple Labs CEO Brad Garlinghouse announced on social media that the company will not pursue a cross-appeal in its case with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). Garlinghouse stated that Ripple is moving on from this chapter to focus on building the Internet of Value, emphasizing that the broader strategy is set.

Previously, BlockBeats reported that U.S. District Judge Analisa Torres in the Southern District of New York rejected a settlement proposal from both the SEC and Ripple for the second time. The proposal aimed to reduce the fine to $50 million and lift a permanent injunction. However, the judge maintained that there is still a possibility of Ripple violating regulations again, necessitating the continuation of the injunction.

data.title: Ripple to abandon cross-appeal in SEC lawsuit
data.description:
Ripple is dropping its cross-appeal, moving to end its years-long legal fight with the SEC.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/ripple-to-drop-cross-appeal-in-sec-lawsuit/
